4caa7a7b-e734-4348-85c1-4994fe6286bc.name: Vanilla Pumpkin Protein Cheesecake
4caa7a7b-e734-4348-85c1-4994fe6286bc.description: Base protein cheesecake (vanilla SF pudding, pumpkin for moisture/body). Launch point for flavor variants. Low-fat / high-protein; pumpkin does the work fat does in a standard cheesecake.
4caa7a7b-e734-4348-85c1-4994fe6286bc.author: user

steps.instructionText: Cook the pumpkin in a nonstick pan over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).
Blend the cottage cheese completely smooth (immersion blender or food processor) - no visible curd, or the cheesecake turns grainy.

Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, and vanilla pudding mix. Mix until smooth (warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps).
Add the eggs and egg white and the cooled pumpkin. Mix just until smooth - do NOT overbeat once eggs are in (air = cracks).
Pour into a parchment-lined 9-inch pan. Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Bake at 325-350F for ~35-45 min, with a tray of hot water on the rack below for moisture. Pull when the edges are set but the center 2-3 in still jiggles (~150F center). Do not overbake - it firms as it cools.
Cool: oven off and door cracked 10-20 min, then counter to room temp ~1-1.5 hr (max 2 hr out), then fridge at least 4 hr - ideally overnight. Best the next day.

0b9c4fe1-c958-49b4-9894-069e0f260ecd.name: Red Velvet Protein Cookies
0b9c4fe1-c958-49b4-9894-069e0f260ecd.description: Crumbl-style protein red velvet cookie with a protein cream-cheese frosting (frosting modeled inline; future sub-recipe candidate). Standalone root - base to be extracted once more Crumbl recipes land.
0b9c4fe1-c958-49b4-9894-069e0f260ecd.author: user

steps.instructionText: Preheat oven to 350F. Line a tray with parchment.
Whisk the wets - yogurt, pumpkin, egg, vanilla, food coloring, vinegar - then stir in the Splenda.
Add the dries - cocoa, oat flour, cornstarch, pudding, protein, baking soda, salt. Mix to a thick dough.
Rest 10 min to hydrate. If still batter-like, add oat flour 1 tbsp at a time until scoopable.
Scoop 5 cookies, flatten slightly (they barely spread). Bake 10-12 min until edges are set but centers still look soft.
Cool fully on the tray - they firm up as they cool.
Beat the yogurt, cream cheese, protein, and Splenda smooth. Add pudding mix to thicken, or chill 20-30 min. Frost the cooled cookies.

ad00bffc-501c-4bc8-a2a3-4bd7ca0f02ac.name: Birthday Cake Protein Cookies
ad00bffc-501c-4bc8-a2a3-4bd7ca0f02ac.description: Crumbl-style birthday cake protein cookie with protein frosting (inline; sub-recipe candidate). Standalone root - sweetener is maltodextrin-based granulated Splenda. Yield set to 6; flip to 8 via edit/scale for the smaller-cookie macro view.
ad00bffc-501c-4bc8-a2a3-4bd7ca0f02ac.author: user

steps.instructionText: Mix all cookie ingredients into a dough. If it is dry, add the almond milk 1 tsp at a time until it just comes together.
Chill 20-30 min.
Scoop onto a lined sheet, wet hands, flatten.
Bake at 350F for 8 min - pull while centers still look soft.
Cool the cookies fully. Beat the frosting ingredients smooth, chill, then frost.

a675c767-d279-4937-8b97-d5e9bebf8853.description: Crumbl-style lemon protein cookie with a powdered-monk-fruit lemon drizzle. FLAGGED FOR TUNING: drizzle flavor underwhelming and macro profile (~13.4 cal/g protein) weaker than target - candidate for future versions (boost protein density, rework the drizzle). Quantities stored in grams (ugly cup measures converted). Standalone root; sweetener is maltodextrin-based granulated Splenda; drizzle monk fruit counted as 0 cal.
a675c767-d279-4937-8b97-d5e9bebf8853.author: user

steps.instructionText: Mix all cookie ingredients into a dough. Add almond milk 1 tsp at a time if dry.
Chill 20-30 min.
Scoop onto a lined sheet, wet hands, flatten.
Bake at 350F for 8 min - pull while centers still look soft.
Cool fully. Whisk the drizzle smooth and spoon over the cookies.

steps.instructionText: Cook the pumpkin in a nonstick pan over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).
Blend the cottage cheese completely smooth (immersion blender or food processor) - no visible curd, or the cheesecake turns grainy.

Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, and vanilla pudding mix. Mix until smooth (warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps).
Add the eggs and egg white and the cooled pumpkin. Mix just until smooth - do NOT overbeat once eggs are in (air = cracks).
Pour into a parchment-lined 9-inch pan. Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Bake at 325-350F for ~35-45 min, with a tray of hot water on the rack below for moisture. Pull when the edges are set but the center 2-3 in still jiggles (~150F center). Do not overbake - it firms as it cools.
Cool: oven off and door cracked 10-20 min, then counter to room temp ~1-1.5 hr (max 2 hr out), then fridge at least 4 hr - ideally overnight. Best the next day.

Add the pumpkin to the same pan and cook 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).
Pour into a parchment-lined 9-inch pan. Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles. Top with graham crumbs + cinnamon (add after baking if you want them crisp).
Finish with sugar-free cinnamon syrup to taste.

c412b1ca-5d17-43bb-8027-56179b41ec88.name: Browned-Butter Protein Cookies
c412b1ca-5d17-43bb-8027-56179b41ec88.description: Browned-butter protein chocolate-chip cookies, makes 3. VERSION A - Thick & Gooey (molten center): tall bakery-style mounds, intentionally uneven (gooey center, firmer edges). The thick middle cooks slowest, so the edges finish before the center - that's the price of a molten center. Soft, fragile, and pale is CORRECT; these don't brown like a normal cookie. Confirm doneness by breaking one open after resting: soft/dense/moist/baked = done; wet/gummy/raw-flour = 1-2 min more. (Version B - Even & Soft-Chewy - is a derived variant that flattens + bakes lower for one consistent texture.)
c412b1ca-5d17-43bb-8027-56179b41ec88.author: user

steps.instructionText: Brown the butter: melt it in a pan over medium heat, swirling, until golden and nutty-smelling (3-4 min). Pour into a mixing bowl and let cool a couple minutes so it won't cook the egg.
Mix wet: whisk in the brown sugar, Splenda, whole egg, egg white, and vanilla until smooth and glossy. Don't whip air in - just combine.

Add dry: stir in the whey, flour, pudding mix, baking soda, and salt until a thick, scoopable dough forms, then fold in the chocolate chips. It should hold its shape, not pour. Too stiff/dry -> a splash of milk; too loose -> a bit more flour. Taste for sweetness.
Chill the dough 30 minutes.
Shape into 3 tall mounds - do NOT flatten.
Bake at 350F / 175C for 9-11 minutes. Pull when the edges are set but the center still looks slightly underdone/soft. Stays pale - don't wait for golden tops.
Rest: leave the cookies on the hot tray 10-15 min after baking - they finish cooking from residual heat and firm up from floppy to chewy as they cool. Don't judge texture until fully cooled.
Fridge (optional): for a firmer, denser, chewier bite, refrigerate the cooled cookies 20-30 min or longer. Protein cookies tighten up when cold. Store leftovers in the fridge.

Chill only ~15 minutes (or let cold dough sit 10 min at room temp before baking). A fridge-cold center lags the most, so less chilling helps the middle keep pace.
Shape into 3 cookies and flatten into discs about 1/2 inch (1.5 cm) thick.
Bake at 325F / 160C on the middle rack for ~11-13 minutes. Pull when set but the centers still feel slightly soft.
Rest: leave the cookies on the hot tray 10-15 min after baking - they finish cooking from residual heat and firm up from floppy to chewy as they cool. Don't judge texture until fully cooled.
Fridge (optional): for a firmer, denser, chewier bite, refrigerate the cooled cookies 20-30 min or longer. Protein cookies tighten up when cold. Store leftovers in the fridge.

2a156707-1edb-4722-9480-57ff233a008f.name: Protein Ice Cream Sandwich
2a156707-1edb-4722-9480-57ff233a008f.description: Healthy protein ice cream sandwiches in the style of Fat Boys: a blended vanilla protein 'ice cream' between chocolate graham crackers. Source: healthbeet.org. Decent as-is; the filling really wants a Ninja Creami churn for texture. Stated macros ~135 kcal / 16C / 13-14P / 3F per sandwich.
2a156707-1edb-4722-9480-57ff233a008f.author: user

steps.instructionText: Blend the protein powder, almond milk, pudding mix, Greek yogurt, vanilla, and salt smooth in a blender.
Freeze ~6 hours, until mostly frozen. Re-churn in a Ninja Creami (best) or re-blend in a food processor.
Line a cake pan with parchment and arrange half-sheets of graham cracker, 6 across the bottom.
Spread the ice cream over the crackers until all are covered.
Top with a second graham cracker and return to the freezer until frozen. Slice into 6 sandwiches.

38ea0850-ba5f-4947-82cc-5db679c02bb1.name: Lucky Charms Protein Cookies
38ea0850-ba5f-4947-82cc-5db679c02bb1.description: Crumbl-style birthday-cake protein cookie with protein frosting (inline; sub-recipe candidate). Ingested without the Lucky Charms marshmallow topping so it reads as a clean base-chassis sibling to the other crumbl cookies. Sweetener modeled as granulated Splenda (matches the crumbl set). Original called for 2 tbsp melted light butter; swapped to 14 g regular butter to match calories at reduced weight. Source: instagram.
38ea0850-ba5f-4947-82cc-5db679c02bb1.author: user

steps.instructionText: In a large bowl, mix all cookie ingredients until combined.
Scoop the cookie dough onto a lined baking sheet.
Wet your hands and flatten the cookies.
Bake at 350F for 8-10 minutes.
While the cookies cool, mix all frosting ingredients in a small bowl until smooth and thick.
Spread the frosting on the fully cooled cookies.

e671240a-fdcf-4245-9444-551981a625f3.name: High-Protein Nanaimo Bars
e671240a-fdcf-4245-9444-551981a625f3.description: A high-protein, lower-calorie take on the classic three-layer Canadian Nanaimo bar: cocoa-coconut base, butterscotch protein filling, baking-chocolate top. Sweetener modeled as granulated Splenda; the 'or 20g oat flour' alt for coconut flour is not taken. Source: instagram. Stated full batch ~1300 kcal / 83C / 118P / 58F (header lists per-bar at 10 bars, but the directions slice 9 — yield set to 9).
e671240a-fdcf-4245-9444-551981a625f3.author: user

steps.instructionText: Toast the shredded coconut ~30 seconds in a nonstick pan until golden brown.
Mix the dry base ingredients (with the toasted coconut), then add the almond milk and egg whites.
Line a baking dish with parchment and press down the base layer.
Mix the filling ingredients to a glossy frosting consistency and spread over the base.
Freeze 20 minutes.
Melt the baking chocolate and coconut oil together in 30-second intervals; sweeten the melt with liquid monk fruit to taste.
Pour and spread the chocolate over the bars.
Set in the fridge ~20 min uncovered, then refrigerate overnight covered. Slice into 9 bars.

67ec8d52-582f-4206-af63-47c2725b9448.name: High-Protein Anti-Pancake Sugar Cookies
67ec8d52-582f-4206-af63-47c2725b9448.description: Low-flour protein sugar cookie tuned to kill the rubbery 'pancake' texture: a scoop of whey replaces most flour and SF instant pudding binds moisture for a dense bakery chew. Sweetener is granulated Splenda; pudding modeled as the Simply Delish SF vanilla already in the library; butter is regular unsalted, already halved to 7 g. Leavening is baking powder only (no soda / cream of tartar). Final step is a fridge rest. Source: user. Stated batch macros ~428 kcal / 44P / 41C / 11F (107/cookie at 4).
67ec8d52-582f-4206-af63-47c2725b9448.author: user

steps.instructionText: Whisk the dry base — AP flour, whey protein, SF pudding mix, Splenda, baking powder, salt — until there are no protein clumps.
Add the melted butter, whole egg, Greek yogurt, and vanilla into the dry mix.
Stir to combine, then stop and let the bowl sit untouched 5 minutes so the whey and pudding starches swell into a shapeable dough. If still tacky, chill 5 more minutes.
Preheat oven/air fryer to 325F. Divide into 4 equal balls on parchment and press down firmly with your palm or a glass — this dough sets in the shape you give it, it won't spread.
Bake at 325F for 6-7 minutes. Pull the moment the edges look dry and set, even if the centers still look slightly soft — whey dries out fast.
Leave the cookies on the hot sheet 5-10 minutes; residual pan heat finishes the centers without making the protein rubbery.
Final step: rest in the fridge ~10 minutes to fully set, then eat.

0c57c6ce-1016-4500-9c95-b03c882c0c6f.name: 20g Protein Frosted Cookies
0c57c6ce-1016-4500-9c95-b03c882c0c6f.description: Macro-friendly frosted protein cookie (Instagram) ingested as a crumbl base-chassis candidate. Notable for a claimed <10 kcal per g protein, achieved by protein-loading: 3 scoops whey in only 5 cookies plus a full cup of nonfat Greek yogurt in the frosting. Modeled with regular AP flour (source said 'Bob's Red Mill GF flour or regular flour'); generic sweetener -> granulated Splenda; the 1 egg white authored as 33 g (one large white) since the library egg-white has no count equivalence; sprinkles ('to taste') omitted so it reads as a clean unfrosted-chassis sibling to the other crumbl cookies. Component keys aligned with the Birthday Cake base so compare row-aligns and promote can graft by key. Not yet made.
0c57c6ce-1016-4500-9c95-b03c882c0c6f.author: user

steps.instructionText: Mix the dry ingredients together — flour, vanilla protein, Splenda, baking powder.
Add the wet ingredients (egg, egg white, melted butter, almond milk, vanilla) to the dry and mix to a dough.
Coat your hands in flour to make the dough easier to shape; divide into 5 cookies on a lined sheet.
Bake at 350F for 10 minutes.
Allow the cookies to cool fully before frosting.
Mix the Greek yogurt, SF vanilla pudding mix, vanilla, and a dash of salt into a frosting; spread on the cooled cookies (add sprinkles to taste).

045b2fad-a2b5-4386-9a73-84262ba75779.name: Crumbl Base Protein Cookie
045b2fad-a2b5-4386-9a73-84262ba75779.description: Flavor-neutral champion base for the crumbl protein-cookie family, synthesized from the set: Birthday Cake (excellent) contributes the soft-crumb cornstarch trick + the inline yogurt/Cool-Whip frosting + the lean chassis; Lemon (excellent) corroborates cornstarch; the 20g Protein Frosted newcomer contributes the protein-loading lever (dialed to a measured 2 scoops, not its texture-risky 3-in-5) and the lean egg white for structure. Vanilla-only — no almond extract / sprinkles / lemon / cocoa, so flavors derive cleanly off it. Cornstarch and egg white are NOT authored here; they are grafted in via promote so the lineage records where each winning component came from. Sweetener is granulated Splenda. Not yet made.
045b2fad-a2b5-4386-9a73-84262ba75779.author: user

steps.instructionText: Mix the dry ingredients into a bowl — flour, vanilla protein, Splenda, baking powder, salt.
Add the wet ingredients — egg, melted butter, Greek yogurt, almond milk, vanilla — and mix to a dough. If dry, add the almond milk 1 tsp at a time until it just comes together.
Chill 20-30 min, then scoop onto a lined sheet, wet hands, and flatten (this dough barely spreads).
Bake at 350F for ~9 minutes — pull while the centers still look soft; they firm up as they cool.
Cool the cookies fully. Beat the frosting ingredients smooth, chill, then frost.

Vanilla-only — no almond extract / sprinkles / lemon / cocoa, so flavors derive cleanly off it. Cornstarch and egg white are NOT authored here; they are grafted in via promote so the lineage records where each winning component came from. Sweetener is granulated Splenda. Not yet made.
d01344d9-3dd4-4ff3-b4ab-06a421307068.author: user
d01344d9-3dd4-4ff3-b4ab-06a421307068.commitMessage: Base: drop egg-white usage (untested import from the 20g newcomer)

steps.instructionText: Mix the dry ingredients into a bowl — flour, vanilla protein, Splenda, baking powder, salt.
Add the wet ingredients — egg, melted butter, Greek yogurt, almond milk, vanilla — and mix to a dough. If dry, add the almond milk 1 tsp at a time until it just comes together.
Chill 20-30 min, then scoop onto a lined sheet, wet hands, and flatten (this dough barely spreads).
Bake at 350F for ~9 minutes — pull while the centers still look soft; they firm up as they cool.
Cool the cookies fully. Beat the frosting ingredients smooth, chill, then frost.

d01344d9-3dd4-4ff3-b4ab-06a421307068.provenanceNote: Cornstarch soft-crumb trick from Birthday Cake + Lemon (both excellent); protein-loading lever + lean egg white from the 20g Protein Frosted newcomer (dialed to 2 scoops to dodge the 3-in-5 pancake risk); Splenda + inline yogurt/Cool-Whip frosting common to the set.

b2d1515e-029b-4ae4-b70e-94396a9cb2f9.name: Crumbl Base Protein Cookie
b2d1515e-029b-4ae4-b70e-94396a9cb2f9.description: The shared base cookie for the crumbl protein-cookie family: the flavor-neutral vanilla chassis every flavor derives from. Built strictly from the tested-excellent set — the Birthday Cake + Lemon dough at 1.5 scoops (your tested ceiling, both rated excellent) plus the cornstarch soft-crumb trick they share — with no flavor identity of its own. The 20g newcomer's protein-load (2.5+ scoops) and lean egg white are kept as queued experiments, not baked into the base. Default frosting is the inline vanilla yogurt/Cool-Whip; per-flavor frostings (cream cheese for red velvet, lemon drizzle, etc.) are meant to swap in.

Add the wet ingredients — egg, melted butter, Greek yogurt, almond milk, vanilla — and mix to a dough. If dry, add the almond milk 1 tsp at a time until it just comes together.
Chill 20-30 min, then scoop onto a lined sheet, wet hands, and flatten (this dough barely spreads).
Bake at 350F for ~9 minutes — pull while the centers still look soft; they firm up as they cool.
Cool the cookies fully. Beat the frosting ingredients smooth, chill, then frost.

d7842659-c10f-459e-a334-5ce41b19097c.provenanceNote: Cornstarch soft-crumb trick from Birthday Cake + Lemon (both excellent); protein-loading lever + lean egg white from the 20g Protein Frosted newcomer (dialed to 2 scoops to dodge the 3-in-5 pancake risk); Splenda + inline yogurt/Cool-Whip frosting common to the set.

97381cf5-add8-4c91-821c-d169ea84a0a0.name: Crumbl Base Protein Cookie
97381cf5-add8-4c91-821c-d169ea84a0a0.description: The shared base cookie for the crumbl protein-cookie family — the flavor-neutral vanilla chassis every flavor derives from. Built from the tested-excellent set: Birthday Cake + Lemon dough at 1.5 scoops (the tested ceiling, both rated excellent) plus their shared cornstarch soft-crumb trick, with a lean egg white kept in for low-risk structure. The 20g newcomer's heavier protein-load (2.5+ scoops) stays a queued experiment, not baked in. No flavor identity of its own; default inline vanilla yogurt/Cool-Whip frosting, with per-flavor frostings (cream cheese for red velvet, lemon drizzle, etc.) meant to swap in. Sweetener is granulated Splenda (calorically modeled, never monk fruit).

steps.instructionText: Whisk the Greek yogurt, butterscotch pudding mix, Splenda, almond milk, and salt smooth. It should be thick and spoonable like a loose caramel — chill 10 min if you want it firmer to swirl.

8c14f9aa-1f51-492e-9c25-bf56efc4cfb2.name: Turtle Real Brown-Sugar Caramel
8c14f9aa-1f51-492e-9c25-bf56efc4cfb2.description: The real, glossy caramel for the top — brown sugar cooked with a little butter and thinned with almond milk, salted. Genuine caramelized-sugar flavor and drizzle (you can't caramelize Splenda), kept deliberately small: ~121 cal for the whole cake, the one honest-sugar indulgence. Sub-recipe (1 batch).
8c14f9aa-1f51-492e-9c25-bf56efc4cfb2.author: user

steps.instructionText: Melt the butter in a small saucepan, add the brown sugar and salt, and cook over medium, stirring, until it bubbles and darkens slightly (~2-3 min). Off heat, whisk in the almond milk (it will sputter) until smooth. Cool slightly — it thickens as it sits; rewarm to drizzle.

7c0ec3e1-c9c4-440b-a045-b273c8439a61.name: Turtle Dark-Chocolate Ganache
7c0ec3e1-c9c4-440b-a045-b273c8439a61.description: Chocolate drizzle for the top — unsweetened baking chocolate melted with granulated Splenda and thinned with warm almond milk. Real cocoa-butter mouthfeel that cocoa powder can't fake; kept to a thin drizzle so the fat stays small (~98 cal for the whole cake). Sub-recipe (1 batch).
7c0ec3e1-c9c4-440b-a045-b273c8439a61.author: user

steps.instructionText: Melt the baking chocolate gently (microwave in 15-sec bursts, or a double boiler). Whisk in the Splenda and the WARM almond milk a little at a time until glossy and pourable — adding it cold can seize the chocolate. Use it warm; it sets as it cools.

steps.instructionText: Cook the pumpkin in a nonstick pan over medium heat, 3-5 min, stirring, until thickened and nutty. Remove and COOL COMPLETELY (hot mix will scramble the eggs).
Blend the cottage cheese completely smooth (immersion blender or food processor) - no visible curd, or the cheesecake turns grainy.

Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, and vanilla pudding mix. Mix until smooth (warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps).
Add the eggs and egg white and the cooled pumpkin. Mix just until smooth - do NOT overbeat once eggs are in (air = cracks).
Pour into a parchment-lined 9-inch pan. Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Bake at 325-350F for ~35-45 min, with a tray of hot water on the rack below for moisture. Pull when the edges are set but the center 2-3 in still jiggles (~150F center). Do not overbake - it firms as it cools.
Cool: oven off and door cracked 10-20 min, then counter to room temp ~1-1.5 hr (max 2 hr out), then fridge at least 4 hr - ideally overnight. Best the next day.

payload.instructionText: Make the crust while the pumpkin cools: stir the graham crumbs, oat flour, cocoa, Splenda, and salt together. Work in the melted butter and egg white until it clumps like wet sand, then press firmly into the bottom of a parchment-lined 9-inch pan — run the parchment up two opposite sides as a sling so you can lift the whole cake out later. Bake at 350F for ~10 min, then cool while you finish the filling.

757d4e4b-3b2b-46c5-b716-400c30724f8a.description: Turtle protein cheesecake derived from the Vanilla Pumpkin base — pecan skipped, so caramel + chocolate carry the turtle identity. Adds a thin graham-forward chocolate crust (graham crumbs + oat flour + cocoa, egg-white bound, minimal butter), a protein caramel swirl marbled into the filling (butterscotch SF pudding + 0% yogurt), and a top drizzle of REAL brown-sugar caramel + unsweetened-baking-chocolate ganache, finished with flaky salt. Filling protein bumped 2 -> 2.5 scoops to hold the macro ratio. Lands ~11 cal/g protein (~210 cal / 19 g protein per slice of 8). Splenda throughout, never monk fruit; the real caramel is the one honest-sugar indulgence, kept small. Untested — first bake pending. Bake in a 9-inch pan with a parchment sling for lift-out.

payload.instructionText: Make the crust while the pumpkin cools: stir the graham crumbs, oat flour, cocoa, Splenda, and salt together. Work in the melted butter and egg white until it clumps like wet sand, then press firmly into the bottom of a parchment-lined 9-inch pan — run the parchment up two opposite sides as a sling so you can lift the whole cake out later. Bake at 350F for ~10 min, then cool while you finish the filling.

Pour half the filling onto the cooled crust. Drop spoonfuls of the protein caramel swirl over it, add the rest of the filling, then drag a knife through in a few S-strokes to marble (don't overmix). Tap the pan on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Once fully chilled (ideally overnight), lift the cake out with the parchment sling onto a plate. Warm the caramel and the ganache separately just until pourable — a few seconds; they should ribbon, not run. Drizzle both over the top in a back-and-forth criss-cross (use a spoon, or a zip-top bag with a tiny corner snipped). Finish with a pinch of flaky salt. Slice with a hot, wiped knife.

2b85692b-bbbc-43a7-8315-c68c12d2ce14.description: Turtle protein cheesecake derived from the Vanilla Pumpkin base — pecan skipped, so caramel + chocolate carry the turtle identity. Adds a thin graham-forward chocolate crust (graham crumbs + cocoa, egg-white bound, minimal butter), a protein caramel swirl marbled into the filling (butterscotch SF pudding + 0% yogurt), and a top drizzle of REAL brown-sugar caramel + unsweetened-baking-chocolate ganache, finished with flaky salt. Filling protein bumped 2 -> 2.5 scoops to hold the macro ratio. Lands ~11 cal/g protein (~210 cal / 19 g protein per slice of 8). Splenda throughout, never monk fruit; the real caramel is the one honest-sugar indulgence, kept small. Untested — first bake pending. Bake in a 9-inch pan with a parchment sling for lift-out.

6c6ae84b-5f6d-4922-89ec-a978e9d14db9.name: Vanilla Pumpkin Protein Cheesecake
6c6ae84b-5f6d-4922-89ec-a978e9d14db9.description: Base protein cheesecake (vanilla SF pudding, pumpkin for moisture/body). Launch point for flavor variants. Low-fat / high-protein; pumpkin does the work fat does in a standard cheesecake. Tuned DENSE (Tier 1+2): pumpkin reduced 120->75g + 1 tbsp cornstarch for a firmer starch set, baked DRY (no water bath) to a just-set center and chilled overnight — denser at near-zero macro cost (~124 cal/slice, ratio 8.8). Stays lean; if a bake comes out rubbery rather than densely creamy, add a little fat (more cream cheese or an extra yolk).

Beat the light cream cheese alone until smooth, then add yogurt, blended cottage cheese, protein powder, sweetener, vanilla, lemon, and vanilla pudding mix. Sift/whisk in the AP flour smooth (no lumps) and mix just until combined — don't overwork it. Warm the bowl ~15 sec if it clumps.
Add the eggs and egg white and the cooled pumpkin. Mix just until smooth - do NOT overbeat once eggs are in (air = cracks).
Pour into a parchment-lined 9-inch pan. Tap on the counter to level and pop bubbles. Top with graham crumbs + cinnamon (add after baking if you want them crisp).

Bake at 325-350F for ~40-50 min — NO water bath; a dry bake sets it denser. Pull when the center is just set with only a slight wobble (~155-160F center), not visibly jiggly. It firms further as it cools — do not chase a fully dry center, that is where a lean cheesecake turns rubbery.
Cool: oven off and door cracked 10-20 min, then counter to room temp ~1-1.5 hr (max 2 hr out), then fridge at least 4 hr — overnight is best and gives the densest, firmest set. Best the next day.
Finish with sugar-free cinnamon syrup to taste.

payload.instructionText: Make the crust while the pumpkin cools: stir the graham crumbs, oat flour, cocoa, Splenda, and salt together. Work in the melted butter and egg white until it clumps like wet sand, then press firmly into the bottom of a parchment-lined 9-inch pan — run the parchment up two opposite sides as a sling so you can lift the whole cake out later. Bake at 350F for ~10 min, then cool while you finish the filling.

Pour half the filling onto the cooled crust. Drop spoonfuls of the protein caramel swirl over it, add the rest of the filling, then drag a knife through in a few S-strokes to marble (don't overmix). Tap the pan on the counter to level and pop bubbles.

Once fully chilled (ideally overnight), lift the cake out with the parchment sling onto a plate. Warm the caramel and the ganache separately just until pourable — a few seconds; they should ribbon, not run. Drizzle both over the top in a back-and-forth criss-cross (use a spoon, or a zip-top bag with a tiny corner snipped). Finish with a pinch of flaky salt. Slice with a hot, wiped knife.

cee5ce40-a9f8-446f-90e7-c573a3a431d3.description: Turtle protein cheesecake derived from the Vanilla Pumpkin base — pecan skipped, so caramel + chocolate carry the turtle identity. Adds a thin graham-forward chocolate crust (graham crumbs + cocoa, egg-white bound, minimal butter), a protein caramel swirl marbled into the filling (butterscotch SF pudding + 0% yogurt), and a top drizzle of REAL brown-sugar caramel + unsweetened-baking-chocolate ganache, finished with flaky salt. Filling protein bumped 2 -> 2.5 scoops to hold the macro ratio. Lands ~11 cal/g protein (~210 cal / 19 g protein per slice of 8). Splenda throughout, never monk fruit; the real caramel is the one honest-sugar indulgence, kept small. Untested — first bake pending. Bake in a 9-inch pan with a parchment sling for lift-out.

b40e8392-d4d5-4272-a10e-bb9fc0c68f58.name: Vanilla Pumpkin Protein Cheesecake
b40e8392-d4d5-4272-a10e-bb9fc0c68f58.description: Base protein cheesecake (vanilla SF pudding, pumpkin for moisture/body). Launch point for flavor variants. Low-fat / high-protein; pumpkin does the work fat does in a standard cheesecake. Tuned DENSE (Tier 1+2): pumpkin reduced 120->75g + 2 tbsp AP flour for a firmer set (AP because that's what's on hand — its gluten sets denser than cornstarch), baked DRY (no water bath) to a just-set center and chilled overnight — denser at near-zero macro cost (~127 cal/slice, ratio 8.9). Stays lean; if a bake comes out rubbery rather than densely creamy, add a little fat (more cream cheese or an extra yolk).
b40e8392-d4d5-4272-a10e-bb9fc0c68f58.author: system
